mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which forces equity valuations lower over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60d returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over 5 years.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which triggers short-term algorithmic buying and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5d returns during early-cycle expansions, with a mean forward return below zero across 100 observations.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 215 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that erode real corporate margins and force delayed monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20d returns remain positive in > 70% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 215.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields a negative mean 20d forward return over a 4-year sample, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate earnings growth, which systematically translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns across multiple business cycles, with Rank IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a contraction in manufacturing output and supply chain bottlenecks, which reduces near-term earnings expectations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances following two consecutive monthly INDPRO declines.
